1994May 12, 1994

🇨🇭 Davos, Switzerland

Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Davos, Switzerland, May 12, 1994 -- May 22, 1994

Meeting URNurn:iso:tc:184:sc:4:meeting:plenary-1994-05-davos-switzerland

16 Resolutions

208Plenary
Approve new AP proposal for Ship Mechanical Systems
SC 4 approves the new AP proposal, ISO/TC 184/SC 4 PMAG N122, Ship Mechanical Systems Application Protocol, and names Jerry Goodwin as the Project Leader.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
207Plenary
Appoint James Fulton as liaison to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 7/WG 11
SC 4 appoints Dr James Fulton as liaison to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 7/WG 11.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
206Plenary
Approve new AP project Plant Spatial Configuration
SC 4 approves the new AP project, Plant Spatial Configuration (ISO/TC 184/SC 4 PMAG N130) with the understanding that it will be closely coordinated with AP 221.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
205Plenary
Affirm EXPRESS language flexibility for data models
SC 4 affirms that the EXPRESS language (ISO 10303-11) should be flexible enough to support the specification of data models regardless of the application area. Requirements for the extension of EXP...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
204Plenary
Organise review of organisational issues
SC 4 requests its Secretariat to organise a review of organisational issues and to make recommendations to SC 4 at its next meeting.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
203Plenary
Organise expert review of technical lessons learned
SC 4 requests PMAG to organise an expert review of the technical lessons learned from the Initial Release of STEP, particularly with respect to the current architecture, methods and practices. The ...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
202Plenary
Require Parts 201 and 203 Abstract Test Suites as CDs
SC 4 directs AP projects for Parts 201 and 203 to deliver their respective Abstract Test Suites as Committee Drafts as soon as possible and not later than March 1995.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
201Plenary
Recommend solution for interoperability of application protocols
SC 4 directs PMAG to recommend a solution for the interoperability of application protocols and circulate it to P-members before the end of 1994.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
200Plenary
Circulate new work item ballot on parametrics
SC 4 instructs its Secretariat to circulate a new work item ballot on parametrics to pursue the goals, activities and workplan presented in the document ISO/TC 184/SC 4/WG 3 N318. NOTE: include rec...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
199Plenary
Encourage WG 8 research on EDIFACT message models
SC 4 encourages the WG 8 Convener to research with the bodies developing EDIFACT message models, the suitable material for manufacturing management requirements. If it is not possible to find such ...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
198Plenary
Perform feasibility study on data modelling in EDI domain
SC 4 directs the WG 8 Convener and the WG 8 P1 Project Leader, to perform a feasibility study to demonstrate how a data modelling methodology can be used in the EDI domain. This study will use STEP...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
197Plenary
Prohibit unauthorized methods for Initial Release development
SC 4 does not authorise the use or development of methods and procedures other than those used to develop the STEP Initial Release, until the methods and procedures for the Initial Release are docu...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
196Plenary
Publish DIS version if corrections are not received
If any of the documents have not been received by the SC 4 Secretariat by 1 September 1994, SC 4 asks its Secretariat to submit the complete Initial Release DIS version of ISO 10303 to the ISO Cent...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
195Plenary
Direct Secretariat to incorporate corrections and publish Initial Release
SC 4 directs its Secretariat to incorporate the corrections developed in response to ballots on Parts 1, 11, 21, 31, 41, 42, 43, 44, 46, 101, 201 and 203. In these documents, only changes of the fo...
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
Acclamation
Acclamation
SC 4 expresses its thanks to Mr Bill Danner for his efforts as the WG 5 Convener in developing the STEP Development Methods and especially for the development of the EXPRESS language.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
Acclamation
Acclamation
SC 4 wishes to thank the Swiss member body for its hospitality and the excellent facilities and support at the May 1994 SC 4 and Working Group meetings in Davos.
ISO/TC 184/SC 4 "Industrial data"
ISO/TC 184/SC 4

Industrial data

Standardization of the content, meaning, structure, representation and quality management of information for engineered products throughout their lifecycle.

Committee

  • Secretariat: ANSI (Secretariat)
  • Chair: Kenneth Swope
  • Established: 1984
  • Standards: 822 published
  • Members: 21 (P), 14 (O)
